title: SUSS Dialogue Session – BSc Events Management
description: Programme discussion with faculty and Q&A for BSc Events Management at SUSS.
longDescription:
This isn't just another hackathon.

Source Awakening is a one-day intensive sprint where creativity meets code, and the next generation of open-source innovators is born.

Led by the Singapore Open Source AI Hub and co-hosted with industry leaders like WaveSpeedAI, Minimax, and memU, this event is your gateway to the future of technology.

We're bringing together the brightest university students, seasoned open-source contributors, and visionary entrepreneurs to build, learn, and connect.

Forget theoretical exercises — here, you will ship a real product in one day.

title: SIM Open House 2026 (Expo)
description: Programme discovery and academic pathway consultation at SIM Open House.
longDescription:
SIM Open House is our key bi-annual event, held in March and September, serving as a vital touchpoint for prospective students and parents exploring their higher education journey.

Event Highlights:
• The SIM Advantage — Degree Pathways, Admissions and Student Success
• Programme Info Sessions
• Programme & Admissions Consultations
• Campus Tours
• Career Talks

title: SUSS ECE Public Lecture Series – Developmentally Aligned AI
description: AI in education and developmentally aligned learning. Part of the SUSS ECE Public Lecture Series.

title: Social Work Academia-Practice Symposium (SWAPS) 2026
venueAddress: SUSS, Block D, Level 1, 463 Clementi Road, Singapore 599494
description: Collaboration between social work research and practice, jointly organised by Allkin Singapore and SUSS SWSD.
longDescription:
Registration for the Social Work Academia-Practice Symposium (SWAPS) 2026 is now open!

Jointly organized by Allkin Singapore and the SUSS School of Social Work and Social Development (SWSD), this year's theme is "Reimagining Social Work Ecosystems: Leadership, Capability-Building and Digital Adaptability".

Guest-of-Honour: Mr Goh Pei Ming, Minister of State for Social and Family Development

Keynote Speakers:

Professor Eunjung Lee, Professor and Endowed Chair in Mental Health & Health at the Factor-Inwentash Faculty of Social Work, University of Toronto. Join us to experience Dr Lee's workshop first-hand, which will explore how practitioners can build their skills through maximising the digital learning environment in SBL. For over 20 years, the Toronto Simulation Programme has been recognised as a global leader in SBL – and the workshop draws on this extensive experience!

Dr Stahl-Wert, Founder and Chairman of World Serving Leaders. Join us to experience Dr Stahl-Wert's keynote and workshop first-hand, where he will:
• Show what best-practice research reveals about organisations that attract, retain, and engage great teams
• Share testimonials that illustrate the behaviours and impact of the best organisations and leaders who serve
• Provide insight and guidance in how to take the first practical steps in growing highly performing teams

Don't miss this opportunity, register today and enjoy the early-bird savings until 13 February 2026.

title: SIM DREAMS Career & Internship Fair 2026
description: Meet ~60 companies, explore internship and career opportunities at SIM.
longDescription:
Meet up to 60 participating companies across diverse industries!

• Gain insights on skills and competencies that employers look out for in potential candidates

Mark your calendar and register now via CareerSense App or through the link qrco.de/DREAMS2026.

See you at the fair!

title: International Leadership Research Forum 2026
description: Leading the Future: Innovating Early Childhood Education for a Changing World.
longDescription:
Leading the Future: Innovating Early Childhood Education for a Changing World

This theme calls for leaders in the early childhood sector to engage with current and emerging complexities and to embrace innovative, inclusive, and relational leadership practices that are responsive to children, families, educators, and communities.

As we navigate a rapidly evolving global landscape — marked by technological transformation, shifting societal norms, and diverse family needs — leaders in early childhood education (ECE) play a vital role in blending timeless principles of early learning with forward-looking tools and approaches that prepare children for an unpredictable future.

This journey encourages us to create inclusive environments that celebrate diversity, use technology to enhance educational experiences while keeping human connections strong, and support policies that nurture the holistic development of children.

ILRF–EC 2026 recognizes the essential role of teachers as co-leaders, change agents, and curriculum innovators in their classrooms and communities. Leadership in ECE cannot thrive without investing in the continuous growth and professional development of educators.

Effective leadership in early childhood also requires building strong partnerships with families, communities, and policymakers to ensure that every child enjoys high-quality and equitable learning opportunities.

By embracing adaptability, empathy, and a lifelong love of learning, leaders in early childhood education are well-equipped to navigate these changing times and lay down a strong foundation for our future generations.

Mark your calendars and register today to secure your spot at the International Leadership Research Forum 2026!

title: Dr Alan HJ Chan Well-Being Conference 2026
description: Redefining success through well-being and lifelong learning.
longDescription:
Dr Alan HJ Chan Well-Being Conference 2026 (Re:Define Success Conference Series)

The Re:Define Success Conference (RDSC) is an annual initiative by the Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), led by the Student Success Centre (SSC).

The series aims to broaden the definition of success by:
• Celebrating diverse aspirations and pathways
• Promoting lifelong learning for continuous growth
• Emphasising holistic development across personal and professional success
• Fostering inclusivity and harnessing diverse talents

The RDSC series contributes to strengthening Singapore's social compact and nurturing a vibrant, caring society that remains globally relevant and successful. This aligns with SUSS' mission of championing lifelong learning and impacting lives for social good.
